steps.title: Factor out the common factor from the target expression
steps.content: We need to find the value of $16 + 8x$. Notice that both terms have a common factor of 8: $$16 + 8x = 8(2 + x)$$

steps.title: Substitute the given value of (2 + x) and calculate
steps.content: From the given equation, we know that $2 + x = 60$. Substituting this into our factored form: $$8(2 + x) = 8(60) = 480$$
